summary:
I have successfully identified and fixed the needle in the haystack. 

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in the `Product.java` file where the `hashCode()` method was incorrectly using `category.hashCode()` instead of `sku.hashCode()`. Since there were only about 10 categories but 10,000 products, this caused severe hash collisions where all products in the same category mapped to the same hash bucket, creating very long collision chains.

## Fix Applied
I modified the `hashCode()` method in `/app/src/Product.java` to use `sku.hashCode()` instead of `category.hashCode()`, which provides proper distribution across hash buckets since SKU is unique for each product.

## Verification
1. Successfully identified the problematic code
2. Made the targeted fix to use SKU for hashing
3. Compiled the Java files successfully
4. Ran `test.sh` which now passes with:
   - Max chain length: 4 (well under acceptable limit of 50)
   - Proper hash distribution across buckets
   - All performance benchmarks met

The fix resolves the performance cliff caused by poor hash distribution and ensures the hash map operates efficiently with evenly distributed entries across buckets.
